seo.title: AI Agent Authentication and Authorization in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent authentication and authorization in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI, AI Agent Authentication and Authorization issues manifest when agents access tools or memory without proper credential scoping, leading to unauthorized data access across sessions. BeeAI's event-driven architecture and Granite model integration exacerbate this, as agent events lack built-in auth validation, allowing privilege escalation through tool injection or memory tampering. Without explicit auth guards in BeeAI's AgentRunner and ToolRegistry, agents can inadvertently bypass organizational boundaries.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent calls `customer_data_tool()` returning data for wrong tenant_id
howItManifests.impact: Cross-tenant data leakage exposing PII across organizations

howItManifests.scenario: Memory state carrying stale auth tokens
howItManifests.symptom: Agent session resumption uses expired JWT from previous user
howItManifests.impact: Impersonation attacks via persistent memory state

howItManifests.scenario: Granite model tool-calling without auth context
howItManifests.impact: Arbitrary API calls to internal services without RBAC

howItManifests.symptom: Vex listener receives full agent memory dump without filtering
howItManifests.impact: Observability tools become unauthorized data exfiltration channels

detection.name: Tenant Context Leak Detector
detection.description: Scans BeeAI agent events for tenant_id mismatches in tool calls vs session context

detection.name: Auth Token Expiration Monitor
detection.description: Validates JWT expiration in BeeAI agent memory state during session resume

detection.name: Unscoped Tool Argument Scanner
detection.description: Detects BeeAI tool calls missing required auth context parameters

fixes.name: BeeAI AuthMiddleware Implementation
fixes.description: Centralized auth validation wrapper for all BeeAI agent events and tools

fixes.name: Secure Tool Registry with RBAC
fixes.description: BeeAI ToolRegistry with automatic auth parameter injection and validation

fixes.name: Session-Scoped Memory Encryption
fixes.description: Encrypt BeeAI agent memory with tenant-specific keys to prevent auth leakage

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Authentication and Authorization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly with BeeAI's event system as a zero-config listener, automatically detecting auth violations across tool calls, memory operations, and Granite model outputs. It captures every AgentRunner event and applies the TenantLeakDetector, TokenExpirationMonitor, and UnscopedToolScanner in real-time, providing instant violation alerts with full event context and automatic remediation workflows.

Vex's BeeAI integration eliminates manual auth auditing by maintaining a baseline of secure session patterns and flagging deviations. When auth violations occur, Vex can automatically inject secure context parameters, block risky tool calls, or trigger session quarantine - all without modifying core agent logic. The `pip install vex-sdk` workflow provides immediate observability into your BeeAI deployment's auth posture.

Production teams use Vex to enforce Zero Trust for AI agents, ensuring every tool call carries proper tenant context and no stale credentials persist across sessions. Vex's ML-powered anomaly detection learns your org's auth patterns and proactively prevents breaches before they cascade through BeeAI's memory and tool layers.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex in 30 seconds to automatically secure your BeeAI agents: `pip install vex-sdk`
